what: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
title: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
text: Single-cell reference mapping Symphony method

title: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
code: RunSymphonyMap(   srt_query,   srt_ref,   query_assay = NULL,   ref_assay = srt_ref[[ref_pca]]@assay.used,   ref_pca = NULL,   ref_harmony = NULL,   ref_umap = NULL,   ref_group = NULL,   projection_method = c("model", "knn"),   nn_method = NULL,   k = 30,   distance_metric = "cosine",   vote_fun = "mean" )

title: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
text: srt_query object class Seurat annotated cell types. srt_ref Seurat object count matrix representing reference object. provided, similarities calculated cells query reference objects. provided, similarities calculated within query object. query_assay assay use query object. provided, default assay query object used. ref_assay assay use reference object. provided, default assay reference object used. ref_pca PCA reduction reference object use calculating distance metric. ref_harmony Harmony reduction reference object use calculating distance metric. ref_umap character string specifying name UMAP reduction reference object. provided, first UMAP reduction found reference object used. ref_group grouping variable reference object. variable used group cells heatmap columns. provided, cells treated one group. projection_method character string specifying projection method use. Options "model" "knn". "model" selected, function try use pre-trained UMAP model reference object projection. "knn" selected, function directly find nearest neighbors using distance metric. nn_method character string specifying nearest neighbor search method use. Options "raw", "annoy", "rann", "cpp". "raw" selected, function use brute-force method find nearest neighbors. "annoy" selected, function use Annoy library approximate nearest neighbor search. "rann" selected, function use RANN library approximate nearest neighbor search. "cpp" selected, function use compiled exact top-k search. provided, function use "cpp" Euclidean cosine distance, otherwise choose search method based size query reference datasets. k number nearest neighbors find cell query object. distance_metric distance metric use calculating pairwise distances cells. Options include: "pearson", "spearman", "cosine", "correlation", "jaccard", "ejaccard", "dice", "edice", "hamman", "simple matching", "faith". Additional distance metrics can also used, "euclidean", "manhattan", "hamming", etc. vote_fun character string specifying function used aggregating nearest neighbors reference object. Options "mean", "median", "sum", "min", "max", "sd", "var", etc. provided, default "mean".

text: Run TriMap (Large-scale Dimensionality Reduction Using Triplets)

title: Run TriMap (Large-scale Dimensionality Reduction Using Triplets) — RunTriMap
code: RunTriMap(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Seurat' RunTriMap(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   features = NULL,   assay = NULL,   layer = "data",   n_components = 2,   n_inliers = 12,   n_outliers = 4,   n_random = 3,   distance_method = "euclidean",   lr = 0.1,   n_iters = 400,   apply_pca = TRUE,   opt_method = "dbd",   reduction.name = "trimap",   reduction.key = "TriMap_",   verbose = TRUE,   seed.use = 11L,   ... )  # Default S3 method RunTriMap(   object,   assay = NULL,   n_components = 2,   n_inliers = 12,   n_outliers = 4,   n_random = 3,   distance_method = "euclidean",   lr = 0.1,   n_iters = 400,   apply_pca = TRUE,   opt_method = "dbd",   reduction.key = "TriMap_",   verbose = TRUE,   seed.use = 11L,   ... )

title: Run TriMap (Large-scale Dimensionality Reduction Using Triplets) — RunTriMap
text: object object. can Seurat object, matrix-like object, Neighbor object, Graph object. ... Additional arguments passed trimap.TRIMAP function. reduction dimensionality reduction use. Default "pca". dims dimensions used. Default NULL. features character vector features use. Default NULL. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. layer layer use. Default data. n_components number TriMap components. Default 2. n_inliers number nearest neighbors forming nearest neighbor triplets. Default 12. n_outliers number outliers forming nearest neighbor triplets. Default 4. n_random number random triplets per point. Default 3. distance_method character string specifying distance metric TriMap. Options : "euclidean", "manhattan", "angular", "cosine", "hamming". Default "euclidean". lr learning rate TriMap. Default 0.1. n_iters number iterations TriMap. Default 400. apply_pca Whether apply PCA nearest-neighbor calculation. Default TRUE. opt_method character string specifying optimization method TriMap. Options : "dbd", "sd", "momentum". Default "dbd". reduction.name character string specifying name reduction stored Seurat object. Default "trimap". reduction.key character string specifying prefix column names TriMap embeddings. Default "TriMap_". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed.use Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Run TriMap (Large-scale Dimensionality Reduction Using Triplets) — RunTriMap

code: RunUMAP2(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Seurat' RunUMAP2(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   features = NULL,   neighbor = NULL,   graph = NULL,   assay = NULL,   layer = "data",   umap.method = "uwot",   reduction.model = NULL,   n_threads = NULL,   return.model = FALSE,   n.neighbors = 30L,   n.components = 2L,   metric = "cosine",   n.epochs = 200L,   cores = 1,   min.dist = 0.3,   set.op.mix.ratio = 1,   local.connectivity = 1L,   negative.sample.rate = 5L,   a = NULL,   b = NULL,   learning.rate = 1,   repulsion.strength = 1,   reduction.name = "umap",   reduction.key = "UMAP_",   verbose = TRUE,   seed.use = 11,   ... )  # Default S3 method RunUMAP2(   object,   assay = NULL,   umap.method = "uwot",   reduction.model = NULL,   n_threads = NULL,   return.model = FALSE,   n.neighbors = 30L,   n.components = 2L,   metric = "cosine",   n.epochs = 200L,   cores = 1,   min.dist = 0.3,   set.op.mix.ratio = 1,   local.connectivity = 1L,   negative.sample.rate = 5L,   a = NULL,   b = NULL,   learning.rate = 1,   repulsion.strength = 1,   reduction.key = "UMAP_",   verbose = TRUE,   seed.use = 11L,   ... )

title: Run UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) — RunUMAP2
text: object object. can Seurat object, matrix-like object, Neighbor object, Graph object. ... Additional arguments passed UMAP. reduction dimensionality reduction use. Default "pca". dims dimensions used. Default NULL. features character vector features use. Default NULL. neighbor name Neighbor object used. Default NULL. graph name Graph object used. Default NULL. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. layer layer use. Default data. umap.method UMAP method used. Options "naive" "uwot". Default "uwot". reduction.model DimReduc object containing pre-trained UMAP model. Default NULL. n_threads Num threads used. return.model Whether return UMAP model. Default FALSE. n.neighbors number nearest neighbors used. Default 30. n.components number UMAP components. Default 2. metric metric function used distance calculations. using string, available metrics : euclidean, manhattan. available generalized metrics : cosine, pearson, pearson2. Note triangle inequality may satisfied generalized metrics, hence knn search may optimal. using metric.function function, signature must function(matrix, origin, target) compute distance origin column target columns. Default "cosine". n.epochs number iterations performed layout optimization UMAP. Default 200. cores spread parameter UMAP, used automatic estimation /b parameters. Default 1. min.dist minimum distance UMAP embeddings, determines close points appear final layout. Default 0.3. set.op.mix.ratio Interpolate (fuzzy) union intersection set operation used combine local fuzzy simplicial sets obtain global fuzzy simplicial sets. fuzzy set operations use product t-norm. value parameter 0.0 1.0; value 1.0 use pure fuzzy union, 0.0 use pure fuzzy intersection. local.connectivity local connectivity, used construction fuzzy simplicial set. Default 1. negative.sample.rate negative sample rate UMAP optimization. Determines many non-neighbor points used per point per iteration layout optimization. Default 5. parameter UMAP optimization. Contributes gradient calculations layout optimization. left NA, suitable value estimated automatically. Default NULL. b parameter b UMAP optimization. Details see parameter . learning.rate initial value "learning rate" layout optimization. Default 1. repulsion.strength numeric value determines, together alpha, learning rate layout optimization. Default 1. reduction.name name reduction stored Seurat object. Default "umap". reduction.key prefix column names UMAP embeddings. Default "UMAP_". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed.use Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

Usually inferred input object. transpose_input Whether transpose input matrix training. with_mean Whether center features scaling. check_expression Whether validate expected CellTypist input format. C Inverse regularization strength logistic regression. solver Optional solver passed CellTypist. max_iter Optional maximum iterations. n_jobs Number CPUs used CellTypist training. use_SGD Whether use SGD training. alpha Regularization strength SGD training. use_GPU Whether use GPU -clustering. Default FALSE. mini_batch Whether enable mini-batch training. batch_number Number batches per epoch mini-batch training. batch_size Batch size mini-batch training. epochs Number epochs mini-batch training. balance_cell_type Whether balance cell types mini-batch training. feature_selection Whether run CellTypist feature selection. top_genes Number top genes used feature selection. date, details, url, source, version Free-text metadata stored model. model_path Optional output path trained model. return Return mode. One "summary", "path", "model". Default "summary". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Train a CellTypist model — TrainCellTypist
text: Depends return: summary list, model path, Python model object.

title: Train a CellTypist model — TrainCellTypist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub)  model_info <- TrainCellTypist(   srt = pancreas_sub,   labels = "SubCellType",   model_path = tempfile(fileext = ".pkl") )  data(panc8_sub) genenames <- make.unique(   thisutils::capitalize(     rownames(panc8_sub),     force_tolower = TRUE   ) ) names(genenames) <- rownames(panc8_sub) panc8_sub <- RenameFeatures(   panc8_sub,   newnames = genenames ) panc8_sub <- standard_scop(panc8_sub)  pancreas_sub <- RunCellTypist(   srt = pancreas_sub,   model = model_info ) CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = c("SubCellType", "celltypist_predicted_labels") )  panc8_sub <- RunCellTypist(   srt = panc8_sub,   model = model_info ) CellDimPlot(   panc8_sub,   group.by = c("celltype", "celltypist_predicted_labels") )  ht <- CellCorHeatmap(   srt_query = panc8_sub,   srt_ref = panc8_sub,   query_group = "celltypist_predicted_labels",   ref_group = "celltype",   width = 4,   height = 3 ) ht$plot } # }

title: The Uncorrected integration function — Uncorrected_integrate
text: Uncorrected integration function

title: The Uncorrected integration function — Uncorrected_integrate
code: Uncorrected_integrate(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch = NULL,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   do_scaling = TRUE,   vars_to_regress = NULL,   regression_model = "linear",   scale_within_batch = FALSE,   linear_reduction = "pca",   linear_reduction_dims = 50,   linear_reduction_dims_use = NULL,   linear_reduction_params = list(),   force_linear_reduction = FALSE,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: The Uncorrected integration function — Uncorrected_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. do_scaling Whether perform scaling. TRUE, function force scale data using Seurat::ScaleData function. vars_to_regress vector variable names include additional regression variables. Default NULL. regression_model regression model use scaling. Options "linear", "poisson", "negativebinomial". Default "linear". scale_within_batch Whether scale data within batch. valid integration_method one "Uncorrected", "Seurat", "MNN", "Harmony", "BBKNN", "CSS", "ComBat". linear_reduction linear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "pca", "svd", "ica", "nmf", "mds", "glmpca". Default "pca". linear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep linear dimensionality reduction. Default 50. linear_reduction_dims_use dimensions use downstream analysis. NULL, estimated dimensions stored linear reduction used available; otherwise, first 50 dimensions used fallback. linear_reduction_params list parameters pass linear dimensionality reduction method. force_linear_reduction Whether force linear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Velocity Plot — VelocityPlot
text: function creates velocity plot given Seurat object. plot shows velocity vectors cells specified reduction space.

title: Velocity Plot — VelocityPlot
code: VelocityPlot(   srt,   reduction,   dims = c(1, 2),   cells = NULL,   velocity = "stochastic",   plot_type = c("raw", "grid", "stream"),   group.by = NULL,   group_palette = "Chinese",   group_palcolor = NULL,   n_neighbors = ceiling(ncol(srt@assays[[1]])/50),   density = 1,   smooth = 0.5,   scale = 1,   min_mass = 1,   cutoff_perc = 5,   arrow_angle = 20,   arrow_color = "black",   streamline_L = 5,   streamline_minL = 1,   streamline_res = 1,   streamline_n = 15,   streamline_width = c(0, 0.8),   streamline_alpha = 1,   streamline_color = NULL,   streamline_palette = "RdYlBu",   streamline_palcolor = NULL,   streamline_bg_color = "white",   streamline_bg_stroke = 0.5,   aspect.ratio = 1,   title = "Cell velocity",   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   theme_use = "theme_scop",   theme_args = list(),   return_layer = FALSE,   seed = 11 )

title: Velocity Plot — VelocityPlot
text: srt Seurat object. reduction dimensionality reduction use. specified, use reduction returned DefaultReduction. dims Dimensions plot, must two-length numeric vector specifying x- y-dimensions cells character vector cell names use. velocity Name velocity use plotting. Default "stochastic". plot_type Type plot create. Can "raw", "grid", "stream". group.Name one meta.data columns group (color) cells . group_palette Name palette use coloring groups. Defaults "Chinese". group_palcolor Colors use coloring groups. Defaults NULL. n_neighbors Number neighbors include density estimation. Defaults ceiling(ncol(srt@assays[[1]]) / 50). density Proportion cells plot. Defaults 1 (plot cells). smooth Smoothing parameter density estimation. Defaults 0.5. scale Scaling factor velocity vectors. Defaults 1. min_mass Minimum mass value density-based cutoff. Defaults 1. cutoff_perc Percentile value density-based cutoff. Defaults 5. arrow_angle Angle arrowheads. Defaults 20. arrow_color Color arrowheads. Defaults "black". streamline_L Length streamlines. Defaults 5. streamline_minL Minimum length streamlines. Defaults 1. streamline_res Resolution streamlines. Defaults 1. streamline_n Number streamlines plot. Defaults 15. streamline_width Width streamlines. Defaults c(0, 0.8). streamline_alpha Alpha transparency streamlines. Defaults 1. streamline_color Color streamlines. Defaults NULL. streamline_palette Name palette use coloring streamlines. Defaults "RdYlBu". streamline_palcolor Colors use coloring streamlines. Defaults NULL. streamline_bg_color Background color streamlines. Defaults "white". streamline_bg_stroke Stroke width streamlines background. Defaults 0.5. aspect.ratio Aspect ratio panel. Default 1. title text title. Defaults "Cell velocity". subtitle text subtitle plot displayed title. Default NULL. xlab x-axis label plot. Default NULL. ylab y-axis label plot. Default NULL. legend.position position legends, one "none", "left", "right", "bottom", "top". Default "right". legend.direction direction legend plot. Can one "vertical" "horizontal". theme_use Theme used. Can character string theme function. Default "theme_scop". theme_args arguments passed theme_use. Default list(). return_layer Whether return plot layers list. Defaults FALSE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Volcano Plot — VolcanoPlot
text: Generate volcano plot based differential expression analysis results.

title: Volcano Plot — VolcanoPlot
code: VolcanoPlot(   srt,   group.by = NULL,   test.use = "wilcox",   res = NULL,   group_use = NULL,   DE_threshold = "avg_log2FC > 0 & p_val_adj < 0.05",   x_metric = NULL,   y_metric = NULL,   palette = "RdBu",   palcolor = NULL,   pt.size = 1,   pt.alpha = 1,   cols.background = "grey80",   cols.highlight = "black",   sizes.highlight = 1,   alpha.highlight = 1,   stroke.highlight = 0.5,   nlabel = 5,   features_label = NULL,   label.fg = "black",   label.bg = "white",   label.bg.r = 0.1,   label.size = 4,   aspect.ratio = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   theme_use = "theme_scop",   theme_args = list(),   combine = TRUE,   nrow = NULL,   ncol = NULL,   byrow = TRUE,   threshold_method = c("rectangular", "hyperbolic"),   hyperbola_c = 6,   annotate_enrichment = FALSE,   enrich_from = c("Enrichment", "GSEA", "GSVA"),   enrich_db = NULL,   enrich_terms = NULL,   enrich_top_terms = 3,   enrich_padj_cutoff = 0.05,   enrich_gsva_score_cutoff = NULL,   gsva_method = NULL,   enrich_nlabel = 15 )

title: Volcano Plot — VolcanoPlot
text: srt object class Seurat containing results differential expression analysis. group.Name one meta.data columns group (color) cells . test.use character string specifying type statistical test use. Default "wilcox". res data.frame data.table differential expression results. res provided, srt ignored. data.frame must contain columns: gene, group1 (factor character), avg_log2FC, p_val_adj, optionally pct.1 pct.2 calculating diff_pct. group_use Groups plot. Default NULL (groups). DE_threshold character string specifying threshold differential expression (used highlight significant genes plot types). Default "p_val < 0.05" sample-level methods ("edgeR" "limma") "avg_log2FC > 0 & p_val_adj < 0.05" otherwise. x_metric character string specifying metric use x-axis (volcano plot). Default NULL, uses "avg_log2FC" sample-level methods ("edgeR" "limma") "diff_pct" otherwise. y_metric character string specifying metric use y-axis. Options: "p_val" "p_val_adj". Default "p_val" sample-level methods ("edgeR" "limma") "p_val_adj" otherwise. palette Color palette name. Available palettes can found thisplot::show_palettes. Default "RdBu". palcolor Custom colors used create color palette. Default NULL. pt.size size points. Default 1. pt.alpha transparency data points. Default 1. cols.background character string specifying color non-DE background points volcano plots. Default "grey80". cols.highlight character string specifying color highlighted points. Default "black". sizes.highlight size highlighted points. Default 1. alpha.highlight transparency highlighted points. Default 1. stroke.highlight stroke width highlighted points. Default 0.5. nlabel integer value specifying number labeled points per group. Default 5. features_label character vector specifying feature labels plot. Default NULL. label.fg character string specifying color labels' foreground. Default "black". label.bg character string specifying color labels' background. Default "white". label.bg.r radius rounding labels' background. Default 0.1. label.size size labels. Default 4. aspect.ratio Aspect ratio panel. Default NULL. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. theme_use Theme use plot. Default "theme_scop". theme_args list additional arguments pass theme function. Default list(). combine Whether combine multiple plots one. Default TRUE. nrow Number rows combined plots. Default NULL. ncol Number columns combined plots. Default NULL. byrow Whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. threshold_method Volcano significance threshold method. Options "rectangular" (legacy DE_threshold) "hyperbolic" (|log2FC * -log10(padj)| > c). Default "rectangular". hyperbola_c Numeric cutoff c hyperbolic volcano threshold. Default 6. annotate_enrichment Whether annotate enrichment-hit genes volcano plots. Enrichment results read existing results srt@tools . Default FALSE. enrich_from Character vector specifying enrichment result source(s) annotate. Options "Enrichment", "GSEA", "GSVA". Default c("Enrichment", "GSEA", "GSVA"). enrich_db Optional database filter enrichment annotation, e.g. "GO_BP" "KEGG". Default NULL. enrich_terms Optional whitelist enrichment term IDs names annotation. Default NULL. enrich_top_terms Number top enriched terms selected per source/group/database. Default 3. enrich_padj_cutoff Adjusted p-value cutoff "Enrichment" "GSEA" annotation. Default 0.05. enrich_gsva_score_cutoff Optional absolute GSVA score cutoff "GSVA" annotation. Default NULL. gsva_method Optional GSVA method filter (e.g. "gsva" "ssgsea") multiple GSVA tool slots exist. Default NULL. enrich_nlabel Maximum number enrichment-derived labels added per group. Labels features_label always retained. Default 15.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) adata <- srt_to_adata(pancreas_sub) adata <- RunPAGA(   adata = adata,   group.by = "SubCellType",   linear_reduction = "X_pca",   nonlinear_reduction = "X_umap" ) srt <- adata_to_srt(adata) srt  # Or convert a h5ad file to Seurat object sc <- reticulate::import("scanpy") adata <- sc$read_h5ad("pancreas.h5ad") srt <- adata_to_srt(adata) srt } # }

code: ccc_to_adata(   srt = NULL,   method = NULL,   condition = NULL,   dataset = 1,   slot.name = "net",   signaling = NULL,   pairLR.use = NULL,   sender.use = NULL,   receiver.use = NULL,   ligand.use = NULL,   receptor.use = NULL,   interaction.use = NULL,   thresh = 0.05,   liana_res = NULL,   score_key = "score",   pvalue_key = "pvalue",   inverse_score = FALSE,   inverse_pvalue = FALSE,   sample_col = NULL,   h5ad_path = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Convert CCC results to OmicVerse communication AnnData — ccc_to_adata
text: srt Seurat object. method Communication result type use. condition Result name comparison name. dataset Dataset index name. slot.name CellChat slot name. signaling Signaling pathway focus . pairLR.use Specific ligand-receptor pair(s) keep. sender.use Sender cell types keep. receiver.use Receiver cell types keep. ligand.use Ligands keep. receptor.use Receptors keep. interaction.use Interaction names keep. thresh Significance threshold used extracting communication results. liana_res Optional precomputed LIANA-like data frame. supplied, srt required. score_key Column liana_res used layers[["means"]]. pvalue_key Column liana_res used layers[["pvalues"]]. inverse_score Whether smaller score_key values converted larger communication strengths. Useful rank-like metrics. inverse_pvalue Whether smaller pvalue_key values inverted writing layers[["pvalues"]]. sample_col Optional column used sample/context/dataset key. NULL, first available column among "sample", "context", "condition", "dataset" used. h5ad_path Optional output path. provided, AnnData object written file returned. verbose Whether print progress messages.

title: Convert CCC results to OmicVerse communication AnnData — ccc_to_adata

title: Seurat v5 fastMNN integration — fastMNN5_integrate
text: Seurat v5 fastMNN integration

title: Seurat v5 fastMNN integration — fastMNN5_integrate
code: fastMNN5_integrate(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch = NULL,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   fastMNN_dims_use = NULL,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   IntegrateLayers_params = list(),   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: Seurat v5 fastMNN integration — fastMNN5_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. fastMNN_dims_use vector specifying integrated dimensions used downstream clustering nonlinear reduction. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. IntegrateLayers_params list parameters passed [Seurat::IntegrateLayers]. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: The fastMNN integration function — fastMNN_integrate
text: fastMNN integration function

title: The fastMNN integration function — fastMNN_integrate
code: fastMNN_integrate(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch = NULL,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   fastMNN_dims_use = NULL,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   fastMNN_params = list(),   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: The fastMNN integration function — fastMNN_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. fastMNN_dims_use vector specifying dimensions returned fastMNN utilized downstream cell cluster finding nonlinear reduction. set NULL, returned dimensions used default. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. fastMNN_params list parameters batchelor::fastMNN function, default empty list. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

Default TRUE. prepare_for_reticulate TRUE (default), coerces X layer matrix CSR float64 Python (avoids invalid dgRMatrix conversion via reticulate). Layers still fail adata_to_srt() skipped reported. Set FALSE plain read_h5ad convert.

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
text: Integrate single-cell RNA-seq data using various integration methods. ChromatinAssay, current workflow uses TFIDF + SVD/LSI preprocessing. setting, Uncorrected supported directly Harmony5 automatically redirected legacy Harmony workflow. Seurat RPCA currently supported ChromatinAssay.

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
code: integration_scop(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   integration_method = c("Uncorrected", "Seurat", "CCA", "RPCA", "scVI", "PeakVI",     "PoissonVI", "WNN", "MultiMAP", "GLUE", "scVI5", "MNN", "fastMNN", "fastMNN5",     "Harmony", "Harmony5", "Scanorama", "BBKNN", "CSS", "Coralysis", "LIGER", "Conos",     "ComBat"),   compute_lisi = FALSE,   lisi_label_colnames = NULL,   lisi_reduction = NULL,   lisi_dims = NULL,   lisi_prefix = NULL,   lisi_tool_name = NULL,   lisi_perplexity = 30,   lisi_nn_method = c("auto", "exact"),   lisi_tol = 1e-05,   lisi_max_iter = 50,   compute_metrics = FALSE,   metrics_batch_col = NULL,   metrics_celltype_col = NULL,   metrics_reduction = NULL,   metrics_cluster_col = NULL,   metrics_tool_name = NULL,   metrics_k_graph = 15,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   do_scaling = TRUE,   vars_to_regress = NULL,   regression_model = "linear",   scale_within_batch = FALSE,   linear_reduction = "pca",   linear_reduction_dims = 50,   linear_reduction_dims_use = NULL,   linear_reduction_params = list(),   force_linear_reduction = FALSE,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   seed = 11,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. integration_method character vector specifying integration method use. Supported methods : "Uncorrected", "Seurat", "CCA", "RPCA", "scVI", "PeakVI", "PoissonVI", "WNN", "MultiMAP", "GLUE", "scVI5", "MNN", "fastMNN", "fastMNN5", "Harmony", "Harmony5", "Scanorama", "BBKNN", "CSS", "Coralysis", "LIGER", "Conos", "ComBat". Default "Uncorrected". ChromatinAssay, prefer "Uncorrected" "Harmony5"; latter automatically switched "Harmony". compute_lisi Whether compute LISI scores integrated result. Default FALSE. lisi_label_colnames Character vector metadata columns used compute LISI. NULL compute_lisi = TRUE, batch used single metadata column name. lisi_reduction Dimensional reduction used LISI computation. Default NULL, uses DefaultReduction() integrated object. lisi_dims Dimensions used lisi_reduction. Default NULL, uses available dimensions. lisi_prefix Prefix used storing LISI metadata columns. Default NULL, uses lisi_reduction. lisi_tool_name Name tool entry used store LISI results. Default NULL, uses paste0(lisi_prefix, "_LISI"). lisi_perplexity Effective neighborhood size used LISI. Default 30. lisi_nn_method Nearest-neighbor backend passed RunLISI(). One "auto" "exact". Default "auto". lisi_tol Tolerance used LISI binary search. Default 1e-5. lisi_max_iter Maximum iterations used LISI binary search. Default 50. compute_metrics Whether compute integration summary metrics selected reduction. Default FALSE. metrics_batch_col Metadata column used batch-mixing metrics. Default NULL, uses batch single metadata column name. metrics_celltype_col Metadata column used biological conservation metrics. Default NULL. metrics_reduction Reduction used integration metric computation. Default NULL, uses DefaultReduction() integrated object. metrics_cluster_col Metadata column used cluster labels celltype_NMI, celltype_ARI, celltype_purity. Default NULL, resolves integrated cluster column automatically possible. metrics_tool_name Name tool entry used store integration metrics. Default NULL, uses paste0(integration_method, "_metrics"). metrics_k_graph Number neighbors used graph-connectivity computation. Default 15. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. do_scaling Whether perform scaling. TRUE, function force scale data using Seurat::ScaleData function. vars_to_regress vector variable names include additional regression variables. Default NULL. regression_model regression model use scaling. Options "linear", "poisson", "negativebinomial". Default "linear". scale_within_batch Whether scale data within batch. valid integration_method one "Uncorrected", "Seurat", "MNN", "Harmony", "BBKNN", "CSS", "ComBat". linear_reduction linear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "pca", "svd", "ica", "nmf", "mds", "glmpca". Default "pca". linear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep linear dimensionality reduction. Default 50. linear_reduction_dims_use dimensions use downstream analysis. NULL, estimated dimensions stored linear reduction used available; otherwise, first 50 dimensions used fallback. linear_reduction_params list parameters pass linear dimensionality reduction method. force_linear_reduction Whether force linear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed integration method functions.

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
text: Seurat object. ChromatinAssay, integrated outputs additionally normalized ATAC naming convention used scop, including *lsi, *UMAP2D, cluster aliases, ATAC_default_* metadata available.

what: A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
title: A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
text: Human pancreatic islet cell datasets produced across four technologies, SMART-Seq2 (E-MTAB-5061), CelSeq (GSE81076), CelSeq2 (GSE85241), Fluidigm C1 (GSE86469), SeuratData package. data set panc8, 200 cells downsampled form panc8_sub dataset.

title: A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
text: Seurat object.

title: A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
code: if (interactive()) {   data(pancreas_sub)   check_r("satijalab/seurat-data")   library(Seurat)   InstallData <- get_namespace_fun("SeuratData", "InstallData")   InstallData("panc8")   data(panc8)   panc8 <- UpdateSeuratObject(panc8)   set.seed(98)   cells_sub <- unlist(     lapply(       split(colnames(panc8), panc8$dataset),       function(x) sample(x, size = 200)     )   )   panc8_sub <- subset(panc8, cells = cells_sub)   counts <- GetAssayData5(     panc8_sub,     layer = "counts"   )   panc8_sub <- CreateSeuratObject(     counts = counts,     meta.data = panc8_sub@meta.data   )   panc8_sub <- panc8_sub[Matrix::rowSums(counts) > 0, ]   panc8_sub <- panc8_sub[toupper(     rownames(panc8_sub)   ) %in% toupper(     rownames(pancreas_sub)   ), ]   panc8_sub$celltype <- gsub("_", "-", panc8_sub$celltype)   panc8_sub$celltype <- gsub(" ", "-", panc8_sub$celltype)   use_data <- get_namespace_fun("usethis", "use_data")   use_data(     panc8_sub,     compress = "xz",     overwrite = TRUE   ) }

title: A subsetted version of mouse 'pancreas' datasets — pancreas_sub
text: scvelo.datasets.pancreas, endocrinogenesis_day15.h5ad

title: A subsetted version of mouse 'pancreas' datasets — pancreas_sub
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if (interactive()) {   library(Seurat)   library(reticulate)   PrepareEnv()   check_python("scvelo")   scv <- import("scvelo")   adata <- scv$datasets$pancreas()   pancreas <- adata_to_srt(adata)   set.seed(98)   cells <- sample(colnames(pancreas), size = 1000)   pancreas_sub <- pancreas[, cells]   pancreas_sub <- pancreas_sub[Matrix::rowSums(     GetAssayData5(       pancreas_sub,       layer = "counts"     )   ) > 0, ]   pancreas_sub[["CellType"]] <- pancreas_sub[["clusters_coarse"]]   pancreas_sub[["SubCellType"]] <- pancreas_sub[["clusters"]]   pancreas_sub[["clusters_coarse"]] <- pancreas_sub[["clusters"]] <- NULL   pancreas_sub[["Phase"]] <- ifelse(     pancreas_sub$S_score > pancreas_sub$G2M_score,     "S",     "G2M"   )   pancreas_sub[["Phase"]][apply(     pancreas_sub[[]][, c("S_score", "G2M_score")],     1,     max   ) < 0, ] <- "G1"   pancreas_sub[["Phase", drop = TRUE]] <- factor(     pancreas_sub[["Phase", drop = TRUE]],     levels = c("G1", "S", "G2M")   )   pancreas_sub$CellType <- gsub("_", "-", pancreas_sub$CellType)   pancreas_sub$CellType <- gsub(" ", "-", pancreas_sub$CellType)   pancreas_sub$SubCellType <- gsub("_", "-", pancreas_sub$SubCellType)   pancreas_sub$SubCellType <- gsub(" ", "-", pancreas_sub$SubCellType)   pancreas_sub@reductions$X_pca <- NULL   pancreas_sub@reductions$X_umap <- NULL   use_data <- get_namespace_fun("usethis", "use_data")   use_data(     pancreas_sub,     compress = "xz",     overwrite = TRUE   ) } } # }

title: scTenifoldKnk Plot — scTenifoldKnkPlot
text: srt Seurat object containing scTenifoldKnk result srt@tools. tool_name Name srt@tools entry created RunscTenifoldKnk. plot_type Plot type. "qq" shows common scTenifoldKnk FC versus theoretical chi-square quantile plot. "effect" ranks top perturbed genes FC. "network" draws knockout-centered WT network subgraph saved tensor networks using thisplot::GraphPlot. "manifold" draws paired WT--KO displacement arrows selected genes non-linear manifold alignment space, showing gene's network context shifts virtual knockout. top_n Number top genes show label. FDR_threshold Adjusted p-value cutoff used mark significant genes. FC_threshold FC cutoff used mark significant genes. features Character vector genes show label. plot_type = "network" "manifold", genes included displayed subset present. label Whether label genes. nlabel Number labels add features = NULL. pt.size Point size. pt.alpha Point transparency. label.size Label text size. edge_top_n Maximum number network edges draw. edge_threshold Minimum absolute edge weight plot_type = "network". NULL, largest edge_top_n edges used. arrow.length Arrow head length plot_type = "manifold". arrow.linewidth Arrow line width plot_type = "manifold". arrow.alpha Arrow transparency plot_type = "manifold". start.pt.size WT-position point size plot_type = "manifold". end.pt.size KO-position point size plot_type = "manifold". manifold.aspect.ratio Panel aspect ratio plot_type = "manifold". Default 1 keeps panel square. Set NULL use device aspect ratio. cols.sig Color significant genes. cols.ns Color non-significant genes. cols.ko Color knocked-genes. xlab, ylab Axis labels. title Plot title. NULL, default title used. theme_use Theme function used style plot. theme_args arguments passed theme_use. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: scTenifoldKnk Plot — scTenifoldKnkPlot
code: data(pancreas_sub) gene_use <- "Pdx1" counts <- GetAssayData5(pancreas_sub, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts") detected <- names(sort(Matrix::rowSums(counts > 0), decreasing = TRUE)) features_use <- unique(c(gene_use, head(detected, 300)))  pancreas_sub <- RunscTenifoldKnk(   pancreas_sub,   gKO = gene_use,   features = features_use,   qc = FALSE,   nc_nNet = 3,   nc_nCells = 200,   td_maxIter = 200,   store_networks = TRUE,   store_manifold = TRUE ) #> ℹ [2026-05-14 07:55:27] Run scTenifoldKnk knockout for "Pdx1" using "cpp" backend #> ℹ [2026-05-14 07:55:27] Construct scTenifoldNet network ensemble #> ℹ [2026-05-14 07:55:27] Denoise network ensemble with tensor decomposition #> ℹ [2026-05-14 07:55:27] Denoise network ensemble with tensor decomposition  ■■■… #> ℹ [2026-05-14 07:55:27] Align WT and KO network manifolds #> ✔ [2026-05-14 07:55:27] scTenifoldKnk results stored in `srt@tools[[scTenifoldKnk]]`  scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "qq")   scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "effect")   scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "network")   scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "manifold")

title: Seurat v5 scVI integration — scVI5_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. scVI_dims_use vector specifying integrated dimensions used downstream clustering nonlinear reduction. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. cores Number DataLoader worker processes scVI training. NULL (default) uses PyTorch default (0, single-process). Increase speed data loading multi-core machines. IntegrateLayers_params list parameters passed [Seurat::IntegrateLayers]. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Standard workflow for scop — standard_scop
text: function performs standard single-cell analysis workflow.

title: Standard workflow for scop — standard_scop
code: standard_scop(   srt,   prefix = "Standard",   assay = NULL,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF = NULL,   do_scaling = TRUE,   vars_to_regress = NULL,   regression_model = "linear",   linear_reduction = "pca",   linear_reduction_dims = 50,   linear_reduction_dims_use = NULL,   linear_reduction_params = list(),   force_linear_reduction = FALSE,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: Standard workflow for scop — standard_scop
text: srt Seurat object. prefix prefix add names intermediate objects created function. Default "Standard". assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether perform normalization. NULL, normalization performed specified assay scaled data. normalization_method method use normalization. Options "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". "SCT" used RNA assay, downstream reductions clustering run generated "SCT" assay. Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. do_scaling Whether perform scaling. TRUE, function force scale data using Seurat::ScaleData function. vars_to_regress vector feature names use regressors scaling step. NULL, regressors used. regression_model regression model use scaling. Options "linear", "poisson", "negativebinomial". Default "linear". linear_reduction linear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "pca", "svd", "ica", "nmf", "mds", "glmpca". Default "pca". linear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep linear dimensionality reduction. Default 50. linear_reduction_dims_use dimensions use downstream analysis. NULL, estimated dimensions stored linear reduction used available; otherwise, first 50 dimensions used fallback. linear_reduction_params list parameters pass linear dimensionality reduction method. force_linear_reduction Whether force linear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

what: A human pancreas Visium spatial example dataset — visium_human_pancreas_sub
title: A human pancreas Visium spatial example dataset — visium_human_pancreas_sub
text: compact gene-filtered version human pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN) 10x Visium dataset GSE254829. object keeps 1986 non-background tissue spots sample GSM8058244 (PanIN-LG2), Spatial assay, slice1 Visium image, tissue coordinates metadata columns x y. Metadata column coda_label stores dominant CODA microanatomical component spot, coda_score stores percentage. Component percentage columns stored coda_ prefix, matched CODA table stored @tools$GSE254829_coda_table. keep package data small directly usable bundled panc8_sub reference, object retains top 5000 genes shared panc8_sub, ranked total spatial counts.

title: A human pancreas Visium spatial example dataset — visium_human_pancreas_sub
text: Seurat object 5000 genes, 1986 spots, one Visium image named slice1.

title: A human pancreas Visium spatial example dataset — visium_human_pancreas_sub
text: Derived GSE254829 human PanIN 10x Visium dataset: GSE254829. package object uses GEO supplementary files GSM8058244_PanIN-LG2.tar.gz GSE254829_codatable_may202024.csv.gz.

title: scop 0.9.0
text: Added ConvertHomologs() homologous feature conversion Seurat, matrix, Matrix objects. function uses GeneConvert() arbitrary Ensembl/biomaRt-supported species pairs, collapses duplicated target homologs summing expression values, preserves Seurat cell metadata spatial images, stores mapping table @tools$ConvertHomologs. Added RunCytoSPACE(), native R/C++ implementation default CytoSPACE spot-level assignment workflow. native backend uses spot-capacity graph construction precomputed Pearson correlation matrices, stores detailed results srt@tools[["CytoSPACE"]], writes summary metadata columns requested prefix. Added SpatialSpotPlot() spatial visualization, including examples show tissue annotations downstream CytoSPACE assignment results. Added shared native progress helper src/log_message.h long-running C++ loops. CytoSPACE assignment, scTenifold tensor decomposition, proportion permutation/bootstrap, sample-level proportion bootstrap now report progress timestamped information style thisutils::log_message(). RunCellphoneDB(): Replaced internal manual homolog-expression conversion path ConvertHomologs(), keeping expression-object conversion behavior consistent across package. GeneConvert() examples now direct expression-object homolog conversion ConvertHomologs() instead showing manual geneID_expand aggregation. Cleaned package-check issues declaring missing namespace imports aligning Rd argument documentation recently updated wrappers. Optional wrapper dependencies checked function entry check_r() instead silently skipping examples adding unnecessary hard dependencies. Updated pkgdown reference grouping spatial analysis, spatial visualization, data conversion, composition-analysis functions. Updated RunCytoSPACE() examples use real bundled data, convert mouse reference data ConvertHomologs(), visualize assignment results. Refreshed examples use bundled real package data removed unnecessary dontrun wrappers examples require Python external command-line tools. Added visium_human_pancreas_sub, Visium human pancreas spatial example dataset (GSE254829) spatial image data CODA-derived annotations, spatial analysis.

title: scop 0.8.9
text: RunGSVA(): Removed redundant dense .matrix() conversion single-cell mode backend = "r"; sparse expression matrices now stay sparse row filtering, reducing peak memory avoiding unnecessary dense materialization. RunCellQC(): Replaced Seurat::SplitObject() lazy cell-name-based subsetting, avoiding full-object duplication per split group. Added caching GetAssay() gene names, GetAssayData5() counts, per-cell QC metrics (nCount / nFeature) outside species-check loop eliminate repeated data extraction. RunDEtest(): Pre-computed cell index mapping (split(names(cell_group), cell_group)) paired-marker tests, replacing per-pair () calls O(1) list lookups. AnnotateFeatures(): Replaced per-detail sapply(… "[") type-stable vapply(…, character(1)) avoid implicit list--character coercion. run_scomm(): Deferred dense conversion reference query subsetting removing premature .matrix() calls; sparse matrices subset first, densified required. RunUMAP2(): Replaced isSymmetric(.matrix(graph)) sparse-native Matrix::isSymmetric(graph) symmetry checks graph subset sampling, avoiding dense materialization large neighbor graphs. RunUMAP2(): Replaced apply(.matrix(graph), 2, order) uwot-predict path internal C++ sparse column top-k helper (run_sparse_topk_by_column()), avoiding full dense conversion column-wise R-level apply(). RunDimsReduction() (PCA centering): Uses SeuratObject::LayerData(…, features = features) read HVF rows scale.data instead loading full matrix followed manual subsetting. RunMDS(): Removed .matrix() Matrix::t(); proxyC::dist now receives sparse matrix directly, avoiding dense conversion large count matrices. integration.R (fastMNN): Removed three .matrix() calls passed batchelor::fastMNN(), accepts sparse matrices natively via SingleCellExperiment. standard_scop(): Replaced full scale.data matrix load (via GetAssayData5()) direct layer access (SeuratObject::GetAssayData Assay5; @scale.data Assay) retrieve rownames checking whether HVFs scaled, substantially reducing peak memory ScaleData decision step. GetAssayData5.Assay(): Fixed parameter naming use positional matching slot/layer argument, GetAssayData5() correctly retrieves counts, data, scale.data layers Seurat v4 (slot) v5 (layer). Previously named layer argument silently dropped SeuratObject v4, always returning data slot regardless requested layer. CSS_integrate(): Added Assay5 guard SeuratObject::JoinLayers() avoid errors Seurat v4 Assay objects, support layered storage. RunDimsReduction(): Added Seurat v4 fallback PCA centering — SeuratObject::LayerData(…, features = …) Assay5-; v4 Assay objects now use GetAssayData5() manual feature subsetting. integration_scop(): Added early detection v5-integration methods (CCA, RPCA, fastMNN5, Harmony5, scVI5) provide clear, actionable error message used Seurat v4 Assay objects, rather failing deep call stack. Added loom_to_srt() pure-R loom--Seurat conversion via rhdf5, preserving velocity-style spliced unspliced layers assays without initializing Python, added Python-backed loom_to_adata() users need AnnData output. Added RunBulk() unified bulk-strategy entrypoint method-vector selection bulk DE, deconvolution, cell-type-specific DE workflows. Added method-specific bulk runners de_limma_voom, de_edgeR_qlf, de_DESeq2, de_dream, deconv_MuSiC, deconv_BisqueRNA, deconv_BayesPrism, csde_TOAST. Standardized bulk results Bulk$results$de, Bulk$results$deconv, Bulk$results$csde, keeping DE outputs compatible existing DEtestPlot(), RunEnrichment(), RunGSEA(), GroupHeatmap(), FeatureHeatmap() data flows. RunBulk(run_enrichment = TRUE, run_gsea = TRUE) now filters bulk DE rows pathway thresholds mirrors successful pathway results Enrichment_Bulk_wilcox GSEA_Bulk_wilcox, EnrichmentPlot() GSEAPlot() can read standard srt@tools contract. Deconvolution CSDE bundles now record computational engine details; current deconvolution runners use explicit backend = "internal" SCOP profile fitting csde_TOAST uses explicit backend = "limma_interaction", native package backends can wired later without pretending call external packages. Added explicit Remotes entries mengxu98/thisplot mengxu98/thisutils source installs can resolve minimum imported versions required SCOP. Optional bulk engines DESeq2 variancePartition installed demand check_r() called lazily get_namespace_fun(). Added method_args expose method-specific tuning parameters without expanding public API surface.

title: scop 0.8.8
text: Updated minimum dependency versions thisplot (>= 0.3.8) thisutils (>= 0.4.5), removed local copies helpers now provided upstream (thisplot::annotate_quadrants(), thisplot::clip_symmetric_range(), thisutils::collapse_sparse_rows()). Updated pkgdown reference grouping cell-cycle workflow. Added optional wrappers RunDorothea(), RunBayesSpace(), experimental RunscTenifoldKnk(). RunscTenifoldKnk() keeps upstream scTenifoldNet workflow fixes QC gene-filter assignment local path, uses native equivalent covariance/downdate path direct sparse matrix construction, selection-based quantile thresholding, controlled per-gene eigensolver parallelism large pcNet() network construction, uses native helpers tensor decomposition, manifold matrix construction, directionality, differential-regulation distance calculations. native tensor-decomposition path now computes MTTKRP updates directly instead materializing four dense unfolding matrices. PrepareEnv(): Added explicit support mamba micromamba executables addition conda, including command-name resolution, automatic package-managed micromamba download conda = "micromamba" requested command PATH, manager-aware logging, micromamba env path detection, ToS handling runs standard conda. Package-managed micromamba environments now use -space cache root avoid micromamba run -p path parsing failures. PrepareEnv() now also stops early reticulate already initialized different Python executable, avoiding unsafe -session switches conda micromamba. Python stack now also pins setuptools < 81 legacy packages trimap can still import pkg_resources. PrepareEnv(): default modules = NULL environment longer includes scomm; install explicitly modules = "scomm" TensorFlow/scOMM stack conflicts default JAX/scVI stack incompatible ml-dtypes requirements. CheckDataList() / standard_scop(): Removed unnecessary -feature ScaleData() call immediately LogNormalize. Downstream workflows still scale selected HVFs PCA, large raw-count inputs longer create dense -gene scale.data intermediate checking. srt_reorder(): Replaced per-cluster repeated sparse subsetting rowMeans() single sparse group-membership matrix multiplication, speeding cluster-reordering step used standard_scop() preserving average expression values. FindExpressedMarkers(): Added sparse fold-change detection-rate prefilter path common sparse RNA inputs, avoiding dense -feature expression materialization marker filtering preserving default results. Supplied features now intersected active layer dense fallback scoring, also keeps scale.data partial-feature layers passing absent rows fold-change calculation. C++-accelerated backends now default available: RunMetabolism(), RunGSVA(), CellScoring(), RunDynamicEnrichment(), RunEnrichment() now prefer backend = "cpp" retaining backend = "r" exact legacy/package behavior. RunPermutation() now uses validated native implementation directly. RunMetabolism() RunGSVA(): Added reusable C++ gene-set scoring backend ssGSEA, zscore, plage; RunGSVA() can now use backend = "cpp" method = "ssgsea", method = "zscore", method = "plage", Gaussian- Poisson-kernel method = "gsva". PLAGE scores oriented gene set mean z-score avoid backend-dependent sign flips. RunMetabolism() RunGSVA(): Added cpp_chunk_size C++ GSVA kernel paths reduce peak dense intermediate memory large cell counts; NULL now auto-selects chunk size large matrices. RunEnrichment(): Added experimental backend = "cpp" ORA path using native hypergeometric implementation faster enrichment tables keeping backend = "r" available clusterProfiler-compatible path. CellScoring(): Added experimental backend = "cpp" support Seurat-style module scoring keeping control-gene sampling R moving sparse mean calculations native code. RunPermutation(): Uses native C++ permutation bootstrap loops directly validating match legacy R calculation observed fractions log2 fold-differences running substantially faster. RunUMAP2(): Added internal C++ sparse column top-k helper Graph inputs speed extraction precomputed neighbor indices/connectivities calling uwot. RunKNNMap() RunKNNPredict(): Added internal C++ dense column top-k helper raw KNN fallback speed nearest-neighbor extraction precomputed distance matrices. PseudotimeProjectionPlot(): Reused internal dense top-k helper pseudotime KNN/gradient neighbor extraction distance matrices. Mapping/integration metrics: Added internal C++ contingency-table helper metric_accuracy(), metric_macro_f1(), metric_purity(), metric_nmi(), metric_ari(), metric_weighted_recall(), collect_mapping_metrics(). RunMetabolism(): Added C++ backend method = "GSVA" keeps Poisson-kernel GSVA scoring path accelerates repeated count-kernel calculations. RunMetabolism() CellScoring(): Added experimental C++ backend AUCell scoring via backend = "cpp" selectable cpp_strategy values ("sparse", "topk", "full"), keeping original R/AUCell implementation available via backend = "r" exact package output. RunDynamicEnrichment() now passes backend AUCell-based scoring. CellScoring(method = "AUCell"): Fixed score-name assignment one feature list scored, avoiding vector dropping metadata column names applied. RunMetabolism(method = "VISION"): Fixed signature construction -memory gene sets passed VISION::Signature objects instead interpreted file paths. DynamicHeatmap() / custom enrichment workflows: Fixed incorrect term label display user-provided TERM2GENE / TERM2NAME use non-standard column names term / name. Custom term annotations now normalized consistently heatmap term labels show readable term names instead fallback IDs (e.g. GO IDs). Related issue #160 (@Pineapple-wen6, @mengxu98). Refactored repeated custom database input handling introducing shared internal helpers normalizing assembling user-provided TERM2GENE / TERM2NAME, applied across RunEnrichment(), RunDynamicEnrichment(), RunGSEA(), RunGSVA(), PrepareDB() keep behavior consistent. integration_scop(): Improved ChromatinAssay handling standardizing ATAC reductions integration, avoiding non-interactive small-batch blocking, clipping TFIDF/rlsi anchor dims available cells, auto-switching Harmony5 legacy Harmony, rejecting unsupported Seurat / RPCA ATAC paths explicit messages.

title: scop 0.8.7
text: Added RunDimsEstimate() DimsEstimatePlot() intrinsic dimensionality estimation reductions, integrated RunDimsReduction() standard_scop() automatically select useful dimensions linear_reduction_dims_use = NULL. Renamed RunDimReduction() RunDimsReduction() updated downstream callers/documentation accordingly. standard_scop(): linear_reduction_dims_use = NULL, now uses estimated dimensions stored reduction (via RunDimsEstimate()) available, fallback first 50 dimensions. Added Seurat v5 integration methods: CCA_integrate(), RPCA_integrate(), fastMNN5_integrate(), Harmony5_integrate(), scVI5_integrate() via Seurat::IntegrateLayers(), exposed integration_scop(). Added Coralysis_integrate() exposed Coralysis integration_scop(), following official Seurat v5 compatible workflow via SingleCellExperiment. Added h5ad_to_srt() reading .h5ad files directly Seurat objects via scanpy.read_h5ad(), automatic CSR/float64 coercion avoid reticulate conversion issues. Layers fail conversion gracefully skipped reported. adata_to_srt(): Improved robustness layer conversion — layer now wrapped tryCatch() individual failures longer abort entire conversion; skipped layers reported warnings. RunCellChat(): Enhanced support condition-specific analyses pairwise merged comparisons via group_column group_cmp parameters. Added RunCellphoneDB() running CellphoneDB cell-cell communication analysis Seurat object official Python package, support species conversion results stored srt@tools[["CellphoneDB"]]. Added RunNichenetr() RunMultiNichenetr() running NicheNet MultiNicheNet analysis Seurat objects standardized result storage. Added unified cell-cell communication plotting functions CCCStatPlot(), CCCHeatmap(), CCCNetworkPlot() CellChat, CellphoneDB, NicheNet, MultiNicheNet results. Remove CellChatPlot(). RunUMAP2(): Fixed reduction lookup check existing reduction names falling back DefaultReduction(), avoiding errors exact reduction name already present.

title: scop 0.8.6
text: Added RunGSVA() gene set variation analysis GSVAPlot() visualization GSVA results. Related issue #146 (@mengxu98). Added RunMetabolism() single-cell metabolism pathway scoring support AUCell, GSVA, ssGSEA, optional VISION, MetabolismPlot() visualization RunMetabolism() now uses scMetabolism KEGG / Reactome metabolism pathway definitions identify metabolism-related terms, rebuilds pathway gene sets updated PrepareDB() annotations, enabling cached database updates cross-species metabolism scoring. Related issue #146 (@mengxu98). Added RunDecontX() integrated optional decontX ambient RNA decontamination RunCellQC(), including contamination metadata, optional decontaminated assay output, threshold-based QC filtering. Related issue #147 (@mengxu98). FeatureStatPlot(): Fixed duplicated X/Y axis titles main title stack = TRUE theme_args title. Stack assembly now strips axis plot titles non-edge panels draws single shared ylab/title via gtable; theme styling theme_args (e.g. axis.title.y, title/plot.title) applied shared grobs. Related issue #145 (@PanSX-Dr). Updated README badge/logo. Removed example datasets ifnb_sub, ref_scHCL, ref_scZCL.

title: scop 0.8.5
text: Default palette changed "Paired" "Chinese". See thisplot::ChineseColors() details. Unified cores parameter across multiple functions (DynamicHeatmap(), FeatureHeatmap(), GroupHeatmap(), heatmap_enrichment()), ensuring cores correctly threaded RunEnrichment(). RunMonocle2() RunMonocle3(): Added xlab ylab parameters, passed internal CellDimPlot() FeatureDimPlot() calls. ListDB(): Now supports multiple species species parameter simultaneously adds Species DB columns output data frame clearer identification. Moved is_outlier thisutils::is_outlier(). configure_apple_silicon_env() (Python function): Added OpenMP compatibility handling macOS arm64 prepending environment lib paths DYLD_FALLBACK_LIBRARY_PATH/DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH preloading libomp.dylib/libiomp5.dylib via ctypes.CDLL(..., RTLD_GLOBAL) reduce scanpy/python-igraph import conflicts. scVI_integrate(): Unified parameter naming renaming num_threads cores consistency across integration functions. Added find_neighbors_and_clusters() run_nonlinear_reduction() helper functions unify operations across integration functions reduce redundant code. TF database now uses AnimalTFDB4 data source. MP database - Web Archive URL year now dynamically determined current date, archive snapshot date MP-related file downloads (VOC_MammalianPhenotype.rpt, MGI_Gene_Model_Coord.rpt, MGI_GenePheno.rpt) extracted server index page ensure consistent versioning. hTFtarget data download URL changed "http://bioinfo.life.hust.edu.cn/static/hTFtarget/file_download/tf-target-infomation.txt" "https://guolab.wchscu.cn/static/hTFtarget/file_download/tf-target-infomation.txt". CSPA data download URL changed "https://wlab.ethz.ch/cspa/data/S1_File.xlsx" "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mengxu98/CSPA/main/S1_File.xlsx". Migrated rliger 2.x workflow (rliger::runIntegration() + rliger::quantileNorm() Seurat object) now prepares/uses ligerScaleData layer via rliger::scaleNotCenter() integration. Updated argument naming/style LIGER_dims_use liger_dims_use, removed legacy quantile-normalization parameter compatibility mapping (ref_dataset), keeping reference supported interface. Optimized installation Python packages Apple Silicon devices. Updated README example code visualizations. regenerating figures, package size reduced ~3 MB.

title: scop 0.8.4
text: FeatureStatPlot() / ExpressionStatPlot(): Fixed box violin x-axis misalignment add_box = TRUE split.. Groups fewer 2 observations now filtered violin density estimation (warning), violin layer uses consistent position_dodge(width = 0.9) match boxplot. Related issue #123 (@oranges7).

title: scop 0.8.3
text: RunDynamicFeatures(): Added PreTSA method dynamic feature fitting. PreTSA algorithm scop re implemented support parallelization higher performance. Original research: PreTSA: computationally efficient modeling temporal spatial gene expression patterns. Use fit_method = "pretsa" B-spline-based piecewise truncated spline analysis; fit_method = "gam" (default) keeps generalized additive models. PreTSA supports knot (0 "auto") max_knot_allowed knot = "auto". Relate issue #133. CellDimPlot() FeatureDimPlot(): Added legend.title parameter (default NULL) control legend title. NULL, default titles used (e.g. group name CellDimPlot, feature empty FeatureDimPlot). ExpressionStatPlot() FeatureStatPlot(): Added legend.title parameter (default NULL) single-legend plots. NULL, default title (e.g. keynm feature/group name) used. FeatureStatPlot() forwards legend.title ExpressionStatPlot(). Moved StatPlot function thisplot::StatPlot. DynamicHeatmap() / heatmap_enrichment(): Fixed incorrect db handling using custom TERM2GENE/TERM2NAME. Enrichment results Database = "custom" incorrectly filtered default db values (e.g. "GO_BP"), causing false “term enriched using threshold” warnings even enrichment succeeded. Relate issue #133 (@1228849000).

title: scop 0.8.2
text: Add DEtestPlot() function, calls original VolcanoPlot() adds two plot types, Manhattan Ring, controlled plot_type parameter (c("volcano", "manhattan", "ring"), default "volcano"). Add standalone functions DEtestManhattanPlot() DEtestRingPlot() direct use. Relate issue #121 (@ericavalentini). Differential expression visualization (DEtestPlot(), VolcanoPlot(), DEtestManhattanPlot(), DEtestRingPlot()): added res parameter accept existing DE results (data.frame). res provided, srt ignored. Data processing supports: (1) group1 cluster column grouped plots; (2) grouping column single panel; (3) gene names row names gene column missing. Relate issue #129 (@mengxu98). PrepareEnv(): Update version parameter specify Python version conda environment. Default "3.11-1" Windows "3.10-1" macOS Unix. Relate issue #103 (@PanSX-Dr). RunNMF(): Add cores parameter RunNMF() optimize printed message. Removed setting Python functions prevents drawing functions causing R crashes. RunPalantir(): Fixed unused plot_format parameter error. parameter now properly excluded arguments passed Python functions. Relate issue #126 (@christinejay990202-dev).

title: scop 0.8.1

Now group.length 1, vector automatically wrapped list; within_groups = TRUE, group_palcolor expanded line group_palette. GroupHeatmap(): group_palcolor fix FeatureHeatmap(): support named-vector input correct expansion within_groups = TRUE. group_by modified group.unify documentation, involving functions: RunCellRank(), RunDEtest(), VolcanoPlot(), RunEnrichment(), EnrichmentPlot(), RunGSEA(), GSEAPlot(), RunPalantir(), RunPAGA(), RunWOT(), RunSCVELO(), releated #120.

RunSCVELO(): Fixed PAGA computation error replacing scv.tl.paga sc.tl.paga (scanpy implementation) better stability. function now uses PAGA implementation RunPAGA() function. RunCellRank(): Fixed GPCCA Schur decomposition error adding fallback mechanism. brandts method fails “subspace_angles” error, function automatically tries krylov method. methods fail, automatically switches CFLARE estimator robust computation. title: scop 0.1.4
text: Update .onAttach(), now .onAttach() print information conda Python. Update PrepareEnv() function easy add update conda environments install Python packages. Added ListEnv() RemoveEnv() functions easy management conda environment Python packages.

title: scop 0.1.3
text: Added TACSPlot() function creating FACS-like plots. Please refer Kernfeld et al. paper Github specific information.

title: scop 0.0.9

title: scop 0.0.6
text: Add GetAssayData5() function, reimplementation GetAssayData(), compatibility Seurat v5 Assay objects. Updated GetFeaturesData() AddFeaturesData() function support retrieving adding feature metadata.
